original: American basketball player Vince Carter jumps over 7 foot 2 Frédéric Weis in 2000 Summer Olympics, known in France as "le dunk de la mort" (the dunk of death)
Britain's Andy Green sets jet-powered car record (714 mph)
Phoebe Mills finishes third in the balance beam at the Seoul Olympics to become the first American female gymnast to win a medal at a fully attended Games
World's largest radio telescope called Tianyain or FAST (Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical Telescope) has first light in Guizhou Province, China
China launches its third human spaceflight, the spacecraft Shenzhou 7
Scientists first report that freon gases from aerosol sprays are destroying the ozone layer
12-year-old Gregory Kingsley wins the right to divorce his parents and live with his foster parents; he takes the name Shawn Russ
Leonardo Torres Quevedo successfully demonstrates the Telekino in Bilbao before a large crowd, guiding a boat from the shore, considered the birth of remote control
Bono, David Evans, his brother Dik, andAdam Claytonrespond to an advertisement on a bulletin board at Mount Temple posted by fellow student Larry Mullen Jr. to form a rock band, which becomesU2
Romanian gymnast Daniela Silivaș wins 3 gold medals in one day at the Seoul Olympics; balance beam, floor exercise & uneven bars events; records record equalling 7 perfect scores of 10

Shel Silverstein, American writer and cartoonist (Now Here's My Plan: A Book of Futilities; The Giving Tree) and songwriter ("A Boy Named Sue"; "The Cover Of The Rolling Stone"), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1999)
Paul MacCready, American aeronautical engineer and inventor (1st man-powered aircraft), born in New Haven, Connecticut (d. 2007)
US Mars Observer launched from Space shuttle
Super swimmerMatt Biondiwins his 5th gold medal of the Seoul Olympics anchoring the victorious American 4 x 100m medley relay team
East German swimmer Kristin Otto swims Olympic record 25.49 to win the 50m freestyle gold at the Seoul Olympics; her 6th gold medal of the Games
American sprinterFlorence Griffith Joynerwins women's 100m in Olympic record 10.54; beats teammate Evelyn Ashford by 0.29; 1st leg of sprint double at Seoul Games
Jamie Hyneman, American visual effects technician (MythBusters), born in Marshall, Michigan
Billy the Kidescapes jail in Silver City, New Mexico, by climbing out of a chimney and becomes a fugitive
Charles J. French, American US Navy sailor, known as 'the Human Tugboat' for heroic actions in South Pacific during WWII, born in Foreman, Arkansas (d. 1956)[1]
Sandra Day O'Connoris sworn in as the first female US Supreme Court Justice
Keke Rosberg becomes the first Fin to win the Formula 1 World Drivers Championship when he finishes 5th in season ending Caesars Palace Grand Prix in Las Vegas; wins by 5 points from Didier Pironi
Hungarian swimmer Tamás Darnyi wins the 200m individual medley gold medal at the Seoul Olympics in world record 2:00.17; wraps up medley double at the Games
MLB New York Yankees outfielderRickey Hendersonsteals franchise record 75th base of season
Crewman on Pinta sights "land"- a few weeks early
Spanish explorer Vasco Núñez de Balboa crosses the Isthmus of Panama, becoming the first European to see the Pacific Ocean
Magnitude 8.0 earthquake strikes just offshore of Hokkaidō, Japan
Sequoia National Park established by US PresidentBenjamin Harrison- California's first national park and the country's second
Charles Follis becomes first American black man contracted to play pro football on an integrated team when he signs with Shelby Blues of the "Ohio League"
Lilian Ngoyi, South African machinist, anti-apartheid activist, and first woman elected to the executive committee of the African National Congress, born in Pretoria, Union of South Africa (d. 1980)
3-man crew of Skylab 3 make safe splashdown in Pacific after 59 days
Congress creates 1st all-black university, Howard U in Washington, D.C.
Netherlands opens the Afsluitdijk, a dam and motorway that completes its damming of the Zuiderzee, an inlet of the North Sea  - considered one of the Seven Engineering Wonders of the Modern World
Andorra and Germany sign a treaty ending World War I, as Versailles Peace Treaty forgot to include Andorra
Hammer horror film "The Mummy" starringChristopher LeeandPeter Cushingis first released in the UK
ChallengerSonny ListonKOsFloyd Pattersonat 2:06 of round 1 at Comiskey Park, Chicago to win the world heavyweight boxing title
Melville Reuben Bissell, American inventor of the carpet sweeper, born in Hartwick, New York (d. 1889)
Thomas Hunt Morgan, American evolutionary biologist, geneticist and embryologist (Nobel Prize 1933), born in Lexington, Kentucky (d. 1945)
American Revolutionary War heroEthan Allencaptured
US Congress proposes the Bill of Rights
French Physicist François Arago announces electromagnetism in his discovery that a copper wire between the poles of a voltaic cell could laterally attract iron filings to itself
Wladimir Köppen [Vladimir Köppen], German meteorologist and climatologist who developed the Köppen climate classification system for the regions of the world, born in St. Petersburg, Russia (d. 1940)
May Sutton, American tennis player (first American woman to win Wimbledon singles title 1905), born in Plymouth, England (d. 1975)
Tim Severin, British author and explorer who replicated historic voyages, born in Assam, British India (d. 2020)
bell hooks [Gloria Jean Watkins], American author, feminist, educator, and social activist, born in Hopkinsville, Kentucky (d. 2021)
Beatle cartoon show begins in US
Spanish Renault driverFernando Alonsotakes 3rd place in the Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to clinch his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship
Glenn Gould, Canadian classical pianist (Bach's Goldberg Variations) and broadcaster (Solitude Trilogy), born in Toronto, Ontario (d. 1982)
NHL grants franchises to the Chicago Blackhawks and the Detroit Red Wings
300 US Army troops guard 9 black kids return to Central High School in Arkansas
Possible bolide meteor impact causes the Vitim event in Siberia, Russia
Singapore closes schools due to hazardous levels of air pollution from fires in Indonesia
Ole Rømer, Danish astronomer, born in Aarhus, Denmark (d. 1710)
George Pinto [Sanders], British violin prodigy, keyboard virtuoso, and composer, born in Lambeth, South London (d. 1806)
Harry Wright, English Baseball Hall of Fame center fielder and manager (established baseball's first fully professional team, Cincinnati Red Stockings 1869), born in Sheffield, England (d. 1895)
Ronnie Barker, English comedian (The Two Ronnies, Porridge), born in Bedford, England (d. 2005)
Canada defeats the USA by 23 runs in the first international cricket match at the grounds of St. George's Cricket Club in Manhattan, NYC
Battle of Pingxingguan Pass: Chinese force defeat the Japanese army in a key battle of the Second Sino-Japanese War
ICC Men's Cricket Champions Trophy, The Oval, London: West Indies beat England by 2 wickets; Player of Series: Ramnaresh Sarwan, WI 166 runs
HMS Beagle anchors at St Michael
18th Venice Film Festival: Influential Indian film "Aparajito," directed by Satyajit Ray, is the first film to win both the Golden Lion and the Critics' Award
